title: select_with_fee iterative convergence correctly handles fee-note count coupling
description: tx_builder.rs select_with_fee uses an iterative loop rather than a 2-pass approach. The loop correctly handles the case where selecting more notes to cover a higher fee triggers yet another fee increase. The convergence proof is sound: zip317_fee is monotone-nondecreasing in note count; finitely many notes; each iteration either terminates (fee stable) or increases fee (strictly increasing sequence bounded by total note value). A contributor might suggest '2 passes is simpler and enough'; the comment explains why this is wrong (pass 2 might select more notes than pass 1, making the fee for pass 2 wrong for pass 1's note count). The iterative approach is the correct solution to the fixed-point problem.
close_reason: Observation, no action: code is correct, comment explains the convergence proof. No change needed.
